title: Onboarding process friction analysis
summary: Fresh external review of TEOF onboarding reveals high completeness but low approachability: 9 entry point docs, immediate cognitive overload with systemic/layer concepts, unclear audience segmentation (human solo vs multi-agent), and no fast evaluation path. Efficiency 4/10, Effectiveness 7/10.
signals: User requested study of onboarding process efficiency and effectiveness. Independent analysis surfaced: (1) documentation fragmentation across 3+ quickstart variants, (2) operator mode requires 4 dense docs before starting, (3) steps 3-7 assume multi-agent coordination even for solo exploration, (4) no tiered path (evaluate → use → coordinate), (5) README 'Start here' section lists 9 links without clear priority.
actions: Document findings in memory reflection and proposal. Recommend: (1) create tiered onboarding (5-min eval / solo dev / multi-agent), (2) consolidate three quickstart docs into canonical path, (3) defer systemic axes/layers/governance to Tier 2+, (4) segment human vs agent paths explicitly, (5) reduce README 'Start here' to single primary doc with progressive disclosure.
notes: Framework design (auditability, determinism, receipts) is sound for stated goals. Barrier is not technical quality but presentation: optimizes for completeness over approachability. Fragmented entry points prevent explorers from evaluating value before commitment. Recommend treating onboarding as S3 (Propagation) + S5 (Intelligence/Learning) work rather than pure S6 (Truth/Documentation).

specific_issues.fragmentation: README:12-21 lists 9 'Start here' links; 3 separate quickstart/onboarding docs with overlapping content; .github/AGENT_ONBOARDING.md is 17-line stub
specific_issues.overload: README introduces 10+ concepts immediately; operator mode (workflow.md:17-23) requires 4 docs before action; heavy jargon without progressive disclosure
specific_issues.audience_unclear: docs/onboarding/README.md:23-47 assumes agent manifest, bus handshakes, claims; no human-solo path
specific_issues.no_fast_path: No '5-minute quick look' for evaluation; 7-step 'first hour' is demanding; multiple receipt checks create friction
specific_issues.critical_path_unclear: Which steps are truly required vs optional? Can solo users skip manifest/bus/claims?
